description: 'lona' is an event in which people gather in one place or exchange for a purpose. It may be planned or informal, and it can take place through an accessible communication medium rather than one room. 'haromi' (meet) is the encounter between participants; 'kowela' (council) is a continuing deliberative body rather than any one meeting.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and round into stressed middle-height back 'o'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and finish with open central 'a'.
usage_notes: The noun can be counted or located like any other event noun. Use 'haromi' when the clause predicates that one participant met another.

semantic_domains.communication: an event organised around exchange among participants
semantic_domains.community: people gathered for a shared purpose or occasion
